channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: GTA 6 Has Multiple Social Media Platforms With Viral Videos & Influencers
item.content:encoded:
Social media is one of the most significant developments in human history in the last couple of decades. GTA 6 is about to go full board on the idea with the game’s own network of social media platforms and interconnected features, it seems.

Do you doomscroll and hate yourself afterwards? Do you cycle through all the different social media apps you’ve got downloaded on your phone? Do you love a viral memworthy video that you watch 100 times in a week, and forget about it the week after?

If any of this applies to you, it seems like Grand Theft Auto 6‘s social media network could be the one for you.

Now, we’ve referenced Brazilian retailer KaBuM! a couple of times recently, as it seems to have GTA 6-specific information that no one else has listed—such as GTA 6 having ‘Improved Performance’ and ‘More Stable FPS’ on PS5 Pro.

It’s unclear if this is a mistake or perhaps AI-generated; we’re not sure.

However, we’ll go on the basis that it’s accurate for now, and the store page says: ‘The big innovation is in the integration of in-game social networks: your character’s cell phone consumes viral videos in real time, allowing you to follow Vice City influencers and discover secret side missions through the networks.’

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6’s Open World is ‘Controlled by Artificial Intelligence’

These details seem to be verified by the game’s Brazilian Amazon page, as well, which advertises:

‘BUILT-IN SOCIAL NETWORKS: Watch viral videos, follow influencers, and discover world events through the game’s mobile.’

As if we didn’t have enough social media in real life. Rockstar Games looks to have created its own addictive network, and we’ll have to see how deep this virtual hive mind goes.

See what this could all entail in GTA 6 Trailer 2.

Are you looking forward to doomscrolling in a video game? Let us know through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out Amazon revealing new GTA 6 features on the product page, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Diablo 4 Warlock Free Trial—Start Date & How To Claim
item.content:encoded:
There’s an absolute wealth of content to get through in Diablo 4, and it’s largely repeatable thanks to the various character classes. As time has passed, more have been added to the game, and one of them is the Warlock class.

It’s widely considered to be one of the more powerful builds in the game, though it is locked behind the Lord of Hatred expansion. That said, Blizzard is offering you the chance to try the character for free, no strings attached.

If you’ve been debating whether to buy the Lord of Hatred expansion and wanted to try the Warlock out for yourself, now might be the perfect time. Here’s when the free trial for the Warlock class begins in Diablo 4, as well as how to claim it.

Diablo 4 Warlock Free Trial Start & End Dates

The Warlock class will be free to all Diablo 4 players from June 30 to July 7, starting and ending at 10 AM PT.

If you’re caught up in the Diablo 4 happenings, you’ll know that the dates coincide with the first week of Season 14: Death Awakening. So, not only will you be able to try the class for free, but you might find yourself hooked on some of the new content!

  • More Insider Gaming: Marvel Rivals Season 9 Release Date, Start Times & Expected Heroes

Of course, you might be from across the world and want to know how these start and end times coincide with your time zone. Worry not, as the dates are all detailed in the table below:

How To Claim Warlock Free Trial In Diablo 4

To claim your Warlock free trial in Diablo 4, all you need to do is log in to the game during that period of time, and create a new character!

You don’t need to worry about redeeming any codes or following any rules; just make a new Warlock and dive into the action.

  • More Insider Gaming: Everything Included In GTA 6 Ultimate Edition & Price

As for what you’ll have access to, you’ll be able to play as a Warlock until you reach level 30, giving you just enough of a taste for the demonic will-bending powers. If you enjoy what you see, just purchase the Lord of Hatred expansion, and your progress will carry forward with the same character.

Since this offer is available during Season 14, check out the new gameplay trailer below:

Will you be giving the Warlock a try? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about the latest regarding GTA 6’s Collector’s Edition, and be sure to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Battlefield 6 Update 1.3.3.0 Details—Changes to Recoil, TTK, Limb Damage and More
item.content:encoded:
Battlefield 6 and REDSEC will receive a new update on June 30, 1.3.3.0, which will bring changes to gunplay depth, Time to Kill (TTK), changes to recoil, limb damage, and more.

The developers of Battlefield 6 have announced a new content series titled “Battlefield Combat,” where they will share their vision for the game’s combat mechanics and their plans for future developments. The first edition of Battlefield Combat will concentrate on gunplay, highlighting the various gameplay changes players can expect in the upcoming update.

What’s New in Battlefield 6 Update 1.3.3.0

Battlefield 6’s update 1.3.3.0 will be implemented across 5 categories: Gunplay Depth, Distance & Drag, TTK, Body Damage Multiplier Changes, and Sniper Sweet Spots. Check out all the changes below:

More Predictable Recoil and increased Bullet Deviation

The first change in Battlefield 6’s update 1.3.3.0 will provide more consistent, predictable recoil direction across all weapons. Additionally, the developers have implemented a slight increase in bullet deviation that players will notice after the first shot. This adjustment will emphasize the importance of controlled bursts or tap-firing to maintain accuracy at longer distances, while allowing full-auto fire to remain effective in close-quarters combat.

Decreased Muzzle Velocity

To keep long-range combat more interesting, the developers will reduce the muzzle velocity of most weapons, particularly SMGs. They have also tuned bullet drag to make anticipating target movement more important.

Time to Kill (TTK)

The developers will reduce the average TTK by adjusting damage multipliers based on where bullets hit the target. This will help retain the best possible TTK.

Body Damage Multiplier Changes

Based on player feedback, the upcoming Battlefield 6 update 1.3.3.0 will reduce the damage dealt to limbs and the lower torso for all weapon classes, except shotguns and sidearms. As a result, players will generally need one additional shot to achieve a kill when primarily hitting the limbs or lower torso. However, headshots will compensate for the reduced damage inflicted on these parts. Future Battlefield Lab tests will focus on changes to body damage multipliers in long-range combat.

Sniper Sweet Spots

Sniper Sweet Spots are areas where the weapon deals increased damage, and if you see a rainbow-tinted Sniper Rifle glint, it means you are in the sniper’s “Sweet Spot.” The developers realized that Sniper Sweet Spot ranges were too generous for snipers, and there was a lot of overlap between all the other guns. Here are the reduced and changed sweet spots for each sniper:

  • SV-98: From 54-90m to 54-75m
  • M2010 ESR: From 75-120m to 75-100m
  • PSR: From 100-150m to 90-120m
  • L115: From 120-175m to 100-133m 
  • Mini Scout: N/A (No “Sweet Spot”; by design, it’s meant for fast follow-up shots)

All the details were available on Battlefield 6’s new blog post.

What are your thoughts on all the combat gameplay changes coming with the Battlefield 6 update 1.3.3.0? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Meccha Chameleon surpassing 10 million copies. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: GTA 6’s Open World is ‘Controlled by Artificial Intelligence’
item.content:encoded:
New information about Grand Theft Auto 6 has been discovered, and it highlights the sort of advance techology being used to power GTA 6. It’s looking like the most sophisticated and alive world Rockstar has probably ever created.

Rockstar Games isn’t exactly a slouch in the perfectionism department. This is why recent releases take so long: The company wants new games to be perfect, if not better.

Even in the two GTA 6 trailers we’ve seen, the world looks jaw-dropping. We’ve since learned that ‘Advanced AI’ is powering all of this.

AI Being Used to Make an Organic GTA 6 Map

The AI conversation has heated up in recent years. But if you’ve ever played a game, you know most use AI in them, so it’s nothing new.

According to Brazilian retailer KaBuM! ‘The GTA VI universe pulsates in a hyper-realistic way,’ and it’s thanks to AI.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 Will Have ‘Improved Performance’ and ‘More Stable FPS’ on PS5 Pro

The product page stated:

‘NPCs (game characters) have their own routines based on advanced AI, generating organic random events around the map. The big innovation is in the integration of in-game social networks: your character’s cell phone consumes viral videos in real time, allowing you to follow Vice City influencers and discover secret side missions through the networks.’

It should be noted that this description doesn’t seem to be offered by any other retailer we’ve seen, and this could be subject to change.

However, active AI should ensure Leonida is the most alive open world to date. Will an alligator randomly roam around the streets before hopping on a skateboard? I hope so.

What do you make of Rockstar using AI to control its world? Let us know through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out Amazon revealing new GTA 6 features on the product page, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Meccha Chameleon Surpasses 10 Million Copies Sold
item.content:encoded:
A new announcement has confirmed that the breakout Steam hide-and-seek title, Meccha Chameleon, has surpassed 10 million copies sold.

Meccha Chameleon has been achieving impressive sales milestones over the past week. On June 20, we learned that it had sold 5 million copies. Just two days later, that number grew to 7 million. Now, only four days after that, the game has reached yet another significant milestone.

Meccha Chameleon Hits 10 Million in the First Month

For those who may not know, Meccha Chameleon was released on June 10, and in just over two weeks, it has sold more than 10 million copies, thanks Genki_JPN. The game has gained significant popularity on social media due to its unique hide-and-seek mechanics. Also, its relatively low price point has contributed to impressive sales in such a short period.

If you haven’t tried out Meccha Chameleon by lemorion_1224, you should definitely give it a shot. In this hide-and-seek game, you embody a chameleon and must blend in with your surroundings. The unique twist is that you need to paint yourself to blend with the environment. At the same time, you need a keen eye to spot players who have painted themselves to blend in, and you can choose to be on the Hiders team or the Seekers team.

RELATED: Is Meccha Chameleon On PlayStation, Xbox Or Nintendo Switch? All Platforms

It is a multiplayer title where you can either enjoy a game of hide-and-seek with your friends or test your skills in a match against strangers in public servers.

What are your thoughts on Meccha Chameleon crossing 10 million copies sold? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about GTA 6 featuring improved performance and more stable FPS on PS5 Pro. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Liquid Confirm trexx and Kicks for VCT EMEA Stage 2
item.content:encoded:
Team Liquid has announced the additions of Nikita ‘trexx’ Cherednichenko and Kimmie ‘Kicks’ Laasner to its Valorant roster ahead of VCT EMEA Stage 2.

The signings follow Wayne ‘wayne’ Chang and Dominykas ‘MiniBoo’ Lukaševičius’s voluntary benchings, which reduced Liquid’s active roster to just three players as Stage 2 loomed.

trexx returns to Liquid as Kicks reunites with former teammate

trexx rejoins Liquid only eight months after leaving the active roster. The Russian helped Liquid to a remarkable VCT 2025 EMEA Stage 2 victory last August, but a disappointing Champions run saw the end of his stint in the active roster.

trexx reunites not only with Liquid, but also with his former Vitality teammate Kicks, who is now promoted from the Team Liquid Academy.

Kicks and trexx managed runner-up at VCT 2024 EMEA Stage 2 and fourth place at Valorant Masters Bangkok, as well as a split victory at VCT 2025 EMEA Kickoff.

While the players themselves will have a natural understanding from playing together, the task to add them to the existing roster may prove more difficult.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

Fans have already expressed concerns surrounding the roles on the roster after the addition of the Initiator-leaning duo. For Liquid, that will have to be the source of quick internal decision-making after the rush to secure any player before the season begins.

Liquid suffered a highly disrupted off-season, which saw wayne and MiniBoo choose to step away from the active roster. With that in mind, a perfect solution to the roster’s roles and synergy may not have been possible.

So it is now for IGL Ayaz ‘nAt’s Akhmetshin to form his team into a workable roster, with Kamil ‘kamo’ Frąckowiak and Semyon ‘purp0’ Borchev expected to step up in responsibility.

Regardless, Liquid’s chance to qualify for Valorant Champions Shanghai is on the line in VCT EMEA Stage 2. The org currently sit joint fifth in the VCT points standing, needing a strong result in the split to make the most important tournament of the calendar.

Let us know what you think of the Liquid lineup on the Insider Gaming Discord


For other news, see the Esports Nations Cup Valorant rosters and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: GTA 6 Will Have ‘Improved Performance’ and ‘More Stable FPS’ on PS5 Pro
item.content:encoded:
A new listing for GTA 6 has revealed that the game will run better on the PlayStation 5 Pro, which is dubbed ‘the most powerful console on the market’.

The news came from a new GTA 6 listing from the Brazilian retailer Kabum.

GTA 6 Runs Best on PS5 Pro

The full description of the PS5 Pro listing reads, “Optimization for PS5 Pro: Enjoy improved performance, more stable frame rates (FPS), and even sharper resolutions on the most powerful console on the market.”

There’s one small catch, though: the PS5 Pro is also the most expensive console on the market right now, coming in at a whopping $900 for those in the US. The latest price increase came in March 2026, and with Xbox raising prices just yesterday, it could also indicate that PlayStation may raise its prices again before the launch of GTA 6.

RELATED – XBOX Announces Another Price Increase for Consoles

That being said, PlayStation is seemingly going all in with its marketing of GTA 6 and dubbing its console the best place to play the game, and with potentially millions of additional consoles being sold because of GTA 6’s release, it’s not surprising.

Other retailer listings have also revealed new key details about GTA 6, including the fact that the game will have a new ‘integrated social network’, where players can “Watch viral videos, follow influencers, and discover world events through your in-game mobile phone.”

Which console will you be playing GTA 6 on? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Bungie studio head stepping down as layoffs emerge. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Marvel Rivals Season 9 Release Date, Start Times & Expected Heroes
item.content:encoded:
New Seasons for Marvel Rivals come thick and fast, and we’re already quickly approaching the beginning of Season 9. With two new Heroes bound to join the ever-growing roster, as well as a new development for the overarching plot, the new Marvel Rivals Season is looking to bring about the Age of Apocalypse.

Of course, Season 8 has been a memorable one, with the introduction of Devil Dinosaur and Cyclops shifting up the meta considerably. That said, it’s bound for more drastic shake-ups, as the arrival of a new season is bound to bring with it a new wave of buffs and nerfs—and that’s without mentioning the new characters.

If you’re excited for the future of Marvel Rivals, here’s when you can expect Season 9 to kick off, including exact start times and a closer look at which heroes could be added to the game.

When Does Marvel Rivals Season 9 Start?

As announced in the Season 8.5 roadmap, the current season will end on July 10, with Season 9 picking up right where it left off.

Each seasonal split seems to last around four weeks now, with an overall reduction in how long each season lasts. With how fast new content is added to the game, you’d think NetEase would need more time between additions, but their release calendar suggests otherwise!

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Earn 2500 Free Units In Marvel Rivals—Summer Festival 2026

That said, with the depths of Marvel’s mythos, it’s not like the studio should run out of content ideas for the hero shooter anytime soon. There are so many avenues to pull from, and there’s still plenty of heavy hitters still to be added to the roster—could any of them be due this Season? Keep reading to find out, but for now, here’s exactly when Season 9 begins.

Marvel Rivals Season 9 Start Times

Judging by previous Marvel Rivals seasons as a guideline, expect Season 9 to begin on July 10, 2026, at 3 AM PT.

A new season is always the most exciting time to play Marvel Rivals, as the community adjusts to the new hero and how they might shake up the meta. That said, if you want to play the new hero day one, you’ll have to be quick on the draw, as thousands of others will have the same idea; get ready to instalock them!

If you want to be there the moment Season 9 goes live, here’s when the update’s due across the world:

Marvel Rivals Season 9 New Heroes

It’s all but confirmed that the two new heroes set to arrive during Marvel Rivals Season 9 will be Jubilee and The Hood.

Thanks to the final Gallery Card of Season 8.5, we know that Apocalypse will be the villain of the month, but in the text, there are distinct hints at two very specific characters. It goes as follows:

A man wearing the cloak of Oblivion … wise-cracking enemy-turned-ally desperate to find a cure for her son’s unqiue ailment, even if it means turning her fireworks against her former friends.

The man wearing the cloak of Oblivion is, of course, referencing The Hood, whilst the firework-spouting lady must be none other than Jubilee. That said, some leaks suggest Jubilee will be her vampire variant from the comics, which might not go down too well with the overall fanbase.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 Sprites, Effects & Rarities

As for their roles, it’s rumored that The Hood will be a Vanguard, whilst Jubilee will be a Strategist, meaning we can finally have a new season with no more Duelists! It’s unconfirmed which hero will arrive in Season 9, or along with the Season 9.5 split, but it’s safe to assume both will arrive in the next few months.

And after Season 9? Well, some leaks suggest the roster all the way until Season 11 has been uncovered, though you’ll have to take them with a pinch of salt. There’s still a huge list of leaked characters yet to arrive in the game, and NetEase still has time to chop and change plans. In the meantime, check out Cyclops’ reveal trailer:

Are you excited for Marvel Rivals Season 9? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about the latest regarding GTA 6’s Collector’s Edition, and be sure to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 10 Games With Peak Player Counts That Defy Belief
item.content:encoded:
The gaming landscape has changed dramatically in the last few decades. In eras gone by, without AAA backing and costly marketing, you simply didn’t stand a chance of cutting through the noise. 

However, in today’s gaming era, little-known oddities can rise to prominence and eclipse the Call of Duty and EA FC powerhouses of the world. Whether through streaming, clever PR, or simply undeniable brilliance, it can all lead to viral, breakout hits that achieve massive peak player counts. 

But, even with that in mind, there are some stories and some numbers that simply defy belief. So, with a little help from Steam DB, here are some mind-boggling peak player counts. 

Meccha Chameleon 

As is often the case, recency bias dictates the first entry. In this case, the flavor of the month hide-and-seek title is Meccha Chameleon. 

Serving as a variation on the popular Prop Hunt game mode made famous by games such as Call of Duty, this title sees players hide and seek, using paint to draw on their stick-figure avatar to blend into the world seamlessly. 

It goes without saying that it’s not always seamless, and this leads to hilarious emergent gameplay moments. Which, in turn, has allowed this game to peak, at the time of writing, at a staggering 340,534 players. 

Cyberpunk 2077

Here’s one that might feel a little bit controversial, but hear me out. Cyberpunk 2077 is a superb game, and perhaps the best Cyberpunk game in existence. But when the game reached its peak of 1,054,388 players, it was far from it. 

The game was a hot mess to say the least, and was even recalled from digital stores to allow CD Projekt Red to make changes. But not before a slew of memes, bugs, and harsh reviews landed on their doorstep. 

Yet, despite that, and the later period of redemption. This release period still serves as the all-time peak. One of the highest concurrent player counts on record. What a bunch of Gonks we were. 

Baldur’s Gate 3 

Again, this feels like an odd inclusion, but allow me to pull this rationale out of my back pocket. CRPGs have always been a very niche interest. They don’t often pull in the mainstream masses. 

Disco Elysium didn’t have a big peak, topping at 8,081. Esoteric Ebb only managed 4,618, and right at the top end, Divinity: Original Sin 2 couldn’t break the 100K barrier. The list goes on, but the point stands: These games tend to be a hard sell to the non-D&D faithful. 

More Insider Gaming: 8 Major Co-op Games Coming in 2026

Yet, Baldur’s Gate 3 managed to buck that trend and become a mainstream darling. Pulling off a jaw-dropping 875,343 concurrent players at its peak. Guess someone at Larian rolled a natural 20 on launch day. 

Windrose 

Sometimes games rise to prominence seemingly out of nowhere, and that was more or less the story for Windrose. After a successful teaser gifted to players at Steam Next Fest, this game’s early access release was a swashbuckling success, pulling in 222,134 players. 

One hell of a feat considering the game is still in active development. But it seems that the game’s blend of seafaring, survival gameplay, and Soulslike combat was too appealing to ignore. 

Much like an elusive treasure, gamers found this one, dug it up from obscurity, and reaped the rewards. Plus, who knows? Come this game’s full release, that peak may rise again. 

Schedule I

I’ll be honest, I still don’t understand why, to this day, Schedule I was quite as popular as it was. But, if I were going to be reductive and boil it down, I would say that it filled the friendslop void in a period where everyone was hungry for their next co-op fix. 

But you do have to give credit where it’s due. Despite some ‘rough around the edges’ qualities, Schedule I is undeniably satisfying to play and thematically memorable. Practically allowing you to live a Breaking Bad-esque fantasy. 

More Insider Gaming: Best Free Games To Play To Beat The Heat

This allowed this drug empire epic to reel in a staggering 459,075. Almost double what fellow plucky indie, Stardew Valley, has ever achieved, just as a point of reference. 

Postal

Next, we have a wacky, wildcard entry coming at you. Many will know Postal as a deeply divisive and controversial series. Something that doesn’t often lead to skyrocketing player counts. Yet, if you take a look, you’ll find that the original Postal had a spike in 2019 of 412,063 players. 

Well, this one is a bit of a red herring. This was due to this game being made free to play, but Valve forgot to disable card drops. Leading to a swarm of bots infesting the game in a bid to get rich quick. 

Postal typically hovers in the double digits when it comes to active players, but for one brief moment, it became rather lucrative to engage with this crude and unpalatable shooter. 

Peak 

We have seen lots of games where the concept is simply to climb to the top of a mountain. Games like Celeste, Cairn, Journey, and more have found success with this format. But none have come close to matching Peak’s peak player count. 

Serving as another friendslop smash hit, Peak bucks the trend of most games in this category by not being anything resembling slop. Peak is a superbly made co-op epic, offering emergent chaos, but with rewarding progression and solid design choices to boot. 

More Insider Gaming: Highest Rated Video Games Of 2025

All of this, along with a brilliantly timed release window, led to this title racking up 170,759 players at its best. A Peak game, at the Peak of its powers, and one that continues to offer multiplayer joy to this day. 

Megabonk 

Ever since Vampire Survivors burst onto the scene, creating the Bullet Heaven genre in the process, gamers have been looking for the next big thing within this hands-off alternative to bullet hell games. 

Many games have come and gone, but by far, the standout competitor everyone took to en masse was Vampire Survivors’ 3D contemporary, Megabonk. 

Vampire Survivors mashed to peak at 77K. Whereas Megabonk achieved a superior 117,336. Does that make it the ultimate bullet heaven experience? I suppose that’s for you to judge. 

Escape from Duckov 

If you’re someone who enjoys games that actively want you to have a terrible time playing them, then you’ll likely be acutely aware of the sadistically addictive nature of Escape from Tarkov. 

Someone took a look at that gritty, grueling series and thought to themselves. This is in desperate need of more ducks, and thus, Escape from Duckov was born. 

More Insider Gaming: 8 Biggest Indie Games Coming in 2026

It’s a much cuter and cuddlier alternative, and this is perhaps why it managed to attract a whopping 301,322 players. Over six times more than its inspiration has ever achieved. Maybe the original isn’t all it’s quacked up to be. 

Palworld 

To say that I was skeptical of Palworld‘s potential success would have been putting it lightly. A game marketed as Pokémon with guns sounded like a shallow gimmick that just wouldn’t deliver. Simply put, I was wrong. 

Palworld offered an open-world creature taming experience that Game Freak had failed to offer time and time again. Rattling Nintendo’s cage so much that a lawsuit would be promptly sent Pocket Pair’s way. 

Perhaps it was more sour grapes than anything. Because if I were a Nintendo rep, reading that all-time high of 2,101,867 players would have driven me to the point of litigation, too.

Which games interest you on this list? Let us know through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming lists, check out these games releasing during the summer lull of 2026, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Callum is the former Editor-in-Chief of Dualshockers, a seasoned video game journalist, and a devoted admirer of all things indie. He loves nothing more than finding an unearthed early-access title…

More from Callum Marshall

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/games-with-peak-player-counts-that-defy-belief/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Riot Games Announce Kick Partnership Bringing LoL, Valorant, TFT, and Wild Rift to the Platform
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has announced a partnership with streaming platform Kick, bringing Riot Games esports to the platform for the first time.

The move follows similar announcements by tournament organizers in the Counter-Strike and Dota space, with ESL now broadcasting its tournaments on the platform.

What will Riot broadcast on Kick?

The announcement is set to include League of Legends, Valorant, TFT, and Wild Rift esports, beginning with League of Legends’ Midseason Invitation (MSI) 2026, which commences on June 28.

Riot explained the motivation behind the partnership, stating, “KICK is the primary platform for many passionate esports fans, especially in LATAM, MENA, and Europe.

Bringing KICK into the mix of our global platform lineup helps us better serve those communities and brings more players together around the moments, matches, and creators they care about most.”

Riot Games added that drops were also coming to the platform to ensure there was no disparity between platforms during broadcast, stating, “The viewing experience on KICK will feel familiar to players, with access to the same drops and incentives available during our global event broadcasts on other platforms.”

Co-streaming has become a predominant, and sometimes controversial, aspect of Riot’s ecosystem, with the co-streaming viewership now outperforming the official broadcast across esports. Nevertheless, Riot is keen to capture co-streamers in the Kick space, opening up co-streaming to Kick creators.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

Does Kick partnership give a clue to Riot’s future intentions?

Kick was co-founded and is co-owned through parent company Easygo Entertainment Pty Ltd by Ed Craven and Bijan Tehrani, the co-founders and co-owners of online casino Stake.

Kick’s admission follows the June 2025 loosening of betting sponsorship restrictions in the LoL and Valorant spaces for organizations, though betting sponsors can still not be shown on broadcast.

The partnership raises the question of whether Riot is moving more in the direction of allowing greater relaxation of gambling restrictions, but for now, the rules remain the same.

Currently, Team Vitality is sponsored by Stake, but displays the French retailer ‘E.Leclerc’ on its jerseys for League of Legends competition due to Riot restrictions.

Additionally, Kick has at times marketed looser moderation as a selling point of the platform; however, Riot specifically stated in their announcement that moderation across Kick would be identical to other platforms, “ensuring a safe and positive experience for all.”

Let us know whether you’ll be watching on Kick on the Insider Gaming Discord


For other news, see our interview with Team Vitality CSO Nicolas Maurer and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
